event: denied routinely irrigating or dilating her neovagina
normally had penetrative sexual intercourse every couple of weeks
had not had intercourse in a few months
CT Abdomen/Pelvis tubular, heterogenous, fluid-filled structure 7.1 cm × 20 cm with blind ending
presented to the local Emergency Department
CT Abdomen/Pelvis increasing inflammatory process in the pelvis surrounding the reconstructed vagina
taken emergently to the operative room
about one liter of purulent fluid drained
preliminary peritoneal culture grew gram-negative rods
peritoneal cultures finalized to Bacterioides thetaioaomicron
peritoneal cultures finalized to Bacteroides caccae
peritoneal cultures finalized to Actinomyces species
additional susceptibilities for the Bacteroides species requested
IV piperacillin-tazobactam prescribed for four weeks
CT Abdomen/Pelvis showed abscess formation, largest next to the liver (15.6 cm)
transcutaneous drainage catheter in pelvis (on CT)
open anterior midline wound with wound vacuum (on CT)
transferred to the intensive care unit
placement of a right perihepatic drain
drained 350 mL from the right perinephric abscess
drained 90 mL from the perisplenic abscess
broad-spectrum PCR sent on drained fluid
antimicrobials narrowed to IV ampicillin-sulbactam at discharge
broad spectrum PCR positive for Gleimia europaea
broad spectrum PCR positive for Alistipes onderdonkil
broad spectrum PCR positive for Varibaculum timonense
broad spectrum PCR positive for Jonquetella anthropi
CT Abdomen/Pelvis decreased size of right and left subphrenic fluid collections
